title: Standardize on utf8mb4 for every text column
description: The legacy utf8 alias stores only three bytes and silently truncates four-byte characters such as emoji.
body: Use when defining text columns or auditing a schema that predates MySQL 8. Do not use the historical `utf8` charset (an alias for utf8mb3); it cannot store four-byte code points, so emoji and many CJK extensions are truncated or rejected. Actions: set CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 and collation utf8mb4_0900_ai_ci (or utf8mb4_unicode_ci on MariaDB) at column, table, and connection level, and verify with SHOW CREATE TABLE plus a round-trip insert of a four-byte sample. Validation signals include the sample emoji surviving a write-then-read and SHOW VARIABLES confirming character_set_client. Risk boundary: converting a large column to utf8mb4 can exceed index key-length limits (767 vs 3072 bytes), so check index prefixes before altering.
